name: cross-file Go signature mismatch

bugs_injected.description: server.Greet calls store.UserStore.Get with the old 1-return-value style, but Get's signature is now (string, bool). The file does not compile, so go test cannot even run. The fix requires (a) reading store/store.go to learn the new signature and the meaning of the bool, and (b) updating Greet to handle both return values — when ok is false, return 'Hello, stranger!'.

notes: The fix is single-file (server.go) but REQUIRES cross-file reading (store.go). If the worker patches server.go without reading store.go, it will make up a signature and the fix will fail to compile. This rung measures: does the worker pull in the needed cross-file context before emitting a patch?
